name: Springfield Housing Authority
description_short: Springfield Housing Authority provides affordable housing programs including public housing, Section 8 vouchers, and new construction projects to low-income individuals, families, seniors, and persons with disabilities.
description_long:
The Springfield Housing Authority (SHA) is a government housing agency serving Springfield and surrounding communities. Founded on the mission of developing and providing quality affordable housing while encouraging economic empowerment, SHA operates under the theme of HOPE (Housing, Opportunity, People, and Empowerment). Executive Director Jackie L. Newman emphasizes the organization's commitment to "changing lives one key at a time" through expanding affordable housing access.

SHA offers three primary housing programs: Affordable Housing (Public Housing) with applications accepted Monday-Tuesday 8:30am-11:30am and 1:00pm-4:00pm; Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8), currently closed for general waitlist but accepting applications for special programs like VASH, FUP, Homeownership, and PBV on Tuesdays only; and new construction projects including Madison Park Place and the RAD 28 Unit Project Conversion at Lincolnwood Estates. The organization actively accepts walk-in tenants for support services on designated days.

SHA distinguishes itself through public-private partnerships focused on growing affordable housing inventory, advocating for policies benefiting low-income constituents, and preserving existing affordable housing stock. The organization specifically serves senior citizens, individuals, families, and persons with disabilities, demonstrating inclusive program design. Their newest RAD conversion project signals commitment to modernizing aging housing stock.

As a government housing authority, SHA provides genuine non-profit affordable housing assistance without lending or credit products. The main limitation is that general Section 8 waitlists are closed, and application windows are narrow and limited to specific days and times. Prospective tenants should verify current program availability before visiting.
pros: Free affordable housing programs—no lending fees or credit products involved
Multiple housing options including public housing, Section 8 vouchers, and new construction projects
Serves vulnerable populations including seniors, families with children, and persons with disabilities
New RAD conversion project (Lincolnwood Estates) providing modernized affordable units
Tenant support services available with dedicated walk-in hours
Executive leadership actively communicating program updates and organizational vision
Public-private partnerships expanding overall affordable housing supply
cons: Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher general waitlist is currently closed to new applicants
Application availability is highly restricted to Monday-Tuesday hours only (8:30am-11:30am, 1:00pm-4:00pm)
Limited tenant walk-in hours may not accommodate working individuals seeking support
best_for: Low-income families and individuals seeking affordable rental housing in Springfield
Senior citizens and persons with disabilities needing subsidized housing
Veterans and special populations eligible for VASH, FUP, or other targeted voucher programs
diagnosis: Springfield Housing Authority is best for low-income families, seniors, and persons with disabilities seeking free or heavily subsidized rental housing in Springfield. The main caveat is that Section 8 general waitlists are closed and application windows are extremely limited to specific weekday hours, requiring significant scheduling flexibility.
services: Affordable Housing (Public Housing) applications and leasing
Madison Park Place new construction leasing
RAD 28 Unit Project Conversion at Lincolnwood Estates leasing
Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for eligible households
VASH (Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing) voucher applications
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
